mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months yield positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases without subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days via wage-driven consumption.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive returns with an accuracy exceeding 52% over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth rates signal weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate revenue trajectories, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors discount near-term earnings.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions exceeding the threshold are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 62% of cases.
